'Optionally rerun individually' may mean that each skipped step can be rerun, but not necessarily immediately. In LTL, we focus on permissible skipping; rerunning is optional and thus not a hard requirement. Therefore, for each such test, when resumeVentilation is selected, globally it is allowed at every step to skip that test. So the formula is: G(resumeVentilation -> G(testOxygenSensorSkip | testLeaksSkip | testPowerSwitchSkip | testPSExpSkip | testFl2Skip | testAlarmsSkip)). This expresses that if resumeVentilation is selected, then in every state it is allowed (possible) to skip any step of the self-test procedure.

explanation:
We are asked to formalize: 'If “Resume Ventilation” is selected, every step of the self-test procedure FUN.6 can be skipped or optionally rerun individually.'

First, let us identify the intent:
- 'If “Resume Ventilation” is selected,' maps to atomic proposition: resumeVentilation
- 'every step of the self-test procedure FUN.6': There are multiple self-test steps, each represented by a skip proposition (such as testOxygenSensorSkip, testLeaksSkip, testPowerSwitchSkip, testPSExpSkip, testFl2Skip, testAlarmsSkip). Each can be skipped or rerun.
- 'can be skipped or rerun individually' means for each self-test step, once resumeVentilation is active, that step is either skipped (skip prop true) or executed (the test can happen, optionally rerun individually).
  - In LTL, 'can be skipped or rerun individually' may be best represented by stating that, if resumeVentilation is selected, for each step, it is always possible (at all times) that either the step is skipped, or, at some point in the future, it is rerun (passed or failed), independently for each step.
- Since the steps can be skipped or rerun, for each self-test step s, after resumeVentilation, it is globally possible to do (skip s now) or (rerun s in the future).

Let each self-test step have two related atomic propositions: s_skip means that step can be skipped, s_rerun means the step is rerun (pass or fail; here we assume rerun = pass or fail detected for this test, for simplicity). For the provided props, we use testOxygenSensorSkip, testLeaksSkip, testPowerSwitchSkip, testPSExpSkip, testFl2Skip, testAlarmsSkip.

Thus, for each s in steps, after resumeVentilation, always (G) it is the case that s can be (optionally) skipped now, or eventually (F) rerun individually (i.e., test pass/fail occurs individually:

For example, for the oxygen sensor step (testOxygenSensorSkip, testOxygenSensorPass/testOxygenSensorFail):
G(resumeVentilation -> G( testOxygenSensorSkip | F (testOxygenSensorPass | testOxygenSensorFail) ))

Combine the same form for each step. For conciseness, let's formalize two steps fully, and the rest would follow analogously.

The overall formula is the conjunction (AND) over each step:
G(resumeVentilation -> G(
  (testOxygenSensorSkip | F(testOxygenSensorPass | testOxygenSensorFail)) &
  (testLeaksSkip | F(testLeaksPass | testLeaksFail)) &
  (testPowerSwitchSkip | F(testPowerSwitchPass | testPowerSwitchFail)) &
  (testPSExpSkip | F(testPSExpPass | testPSExpFail)) &
  (testFl2Skip | F(testFl2Pass | testFl2Fail)) &
  (testAlarmsSkip | F(testAlarmsPass | testAlarmsFail))
))

Here, G distributes over the conjunction for each step. This means: if resumeVentilation is selected, for each test, globally, it is always the case that it can be skipped or rerun individually (eventually in the future).

explanation: First, 'If “Resume Ventilation” is selected' is mapped to the atomic proposition resumeVentilation. 'Every step of the self-test procedure FUN.6 can be skipped or optionally rerun individually' means that for each step in the set of self-test steps (which likely correspond to skip-test propositions for each subtest of FUN.6), after resuming ventilation, at every moment, each such test can either be skipped or rerun. We only have the skip/test propositions in the atomic set: testOxygenSensorSkip, testLeaksSkip, testFl2Skip, testAlarmsSkip, testPowerSwitchSkip, testPSExpSkip. To represent that after resumeVentilation, at every moment, each step can either be skipped or rerun, we use G (globally) and F (finally/eventually). However, since the user says 'every step can be skipped or optionally rerun individually', this corresponds to for all steps: G after ResumeVentilation, at every moment, the step can either be skipped (skip proposition holds at that step), or the test passes/fails/gets rerun (but in LTL, we only have skip, so permission to skip). So, the property is: after resumeVentilation, for each step s, at every point, it is possible that s is skipped (skip proposition holds), but skipping is always allowed, not required. In other words, G(resumeVentilation -> G(skip_s)), for each step s. But the natural language is permissive, not mandatory. So, if resumeVentilation, then for each step, skipping or rerunning is allowed; thus, skipping is always possible, never forbidden. In LTL, permission is difficult, so we say: it's never mandatory to perform a test, i.e., it's always possible to skip. A simple permissibility encoding in LTL is: G(resumeVentilation -> G(skip_s ∨ rerun_s)), but without rerun_s atomic propositions, we just allow skipping at any time. Thus, the best faithful translation with the available atomic propositions is: For each test x: G(resumeVentilation -> G(testXSkip)), for all testXSkip in the set. If there were rerun test atomic propositions, we would include them as permitted too.

The core sentence is: 'If “Resume Ventilation” is selected, every step of the self-test procedure FUN.6 can be skipped or optionally rerun individually.' 
- 'If “Resume Ventilation” is selected' maps to the atomic proposition resumeVentilation.
- 'self-test procedure steps can be skipped' can be represented by atomic propositions that indicate skipping specific self-test steps. For illustration, we'll consider: testOxygenSensorSkip, testLeaksSkip, testPowerSwitchSkip, testPSExpSkip, testAlarmsSkip, testFl2Skip as the set of skip options for the different steps of FUN.6.
- 'can be skipped or optionally rerun individually': Since LTL expresses what must always (or may always) be possible, we translate this as: As long as resumeVentilation is true, at every time in the future, the system can (is allowed to) skip any of the self-test steps (i.e., skipping is allowed, and there's no requirement to run all steps in sequence). This 'allowed' is interpreted as 'possibly', i.e., it's always possible (globally possible) that any individual skip may eventually happen, for each step.
- The logic is: If resumeVentilation is selected, then at every moment (globally), for each self-test step, it is always possible (i.e., not forbidden) that the user could skip (or rerun) that step. In LTL, we express this by: G(resumeVentilation -> (F testOxygenSensorSkip & F testLeaksSkip & F testPowerSwitchSkip & F testPSExpSkip & F testAlarmsSkip & F testFl2Skip))
- We assume 'optionally rerun individually' is permitted by the same formula (permitting skipping at any time); stricter semantics would require auxiliary propositions for rerun and more detailed modeling of user actions, which is not in the provided atomic propositions.

Thus, the final formula is: G(resumeVentilation -> (F testOxygenSensorSkip & F testLeaksSkip & F testPowerSwitchSkip & F testPSExpSkip & F testAlarmsSkip & F testFl2Skip))
